medications.name: Acetaminophen
medications.usedFor: Headache, fever, minor aches, sore throat pain when inflammation control is not required
medications.sideEffects: Usually mild; nausea or rash can occur; too much can cause severe liver damage
medications.doNotUseIf: Severe liver disease, heavy alcohol use, or already taking another product with acetaminophen/APAP
medications.interactions: Many cold/flu combo products contain acetaminophen; avoid doubling up
medications.seekCareIf: Pain/fever worsens, lasts more than a few days, rash/swelling appears, or overdose is possible

medications.name: Adapalene topical 0.1%
medications.usedFor: Comedonal/inflammatory acne prevention and treatment
medications.sideEffects: Dryness, redness, peeling, burning, sun sensitivity
medications.doNotUseIf: Pregnancy/planning pregnancy unless clinician approves, eczema/sunburned skin, severe acne needing clinician
medications.interactions: Use sunscreen; avoid harsh scrubs/peels at first
medications.seekCareIf: Severe irritation, swelling, allergic reaction

medications.name: Artificial tears / ocular lubricants
medications.commonExamples: Refresh, Systane, TheraTears, store-brand artificial tears
medications.usedFor: Dry, gritty, irritated eyes without injury/infection red flags
medications.sideEffects: Temporary blurred vision, mild stinging
medications.doNotUseIf: Eye pain, vision changes, eye injury, infection signs, severe redness
medications.interactions: Preservative-free may be preferred for frequent use; avoid touching dropper tip
medications.seekCareIf: Eye pain, vision changes, redness persists/worsens

medications.symptomUseCase: Pain, fever; aspirin-specific uses require clinician guidance
medications.name: Aspirin
medications.usedFor: Minor pain/fever in adults; low-dose heart protection only when recommended by clinician
medications.sideEffects: Stomach upset, heartburn, ringing in ears; bleeding risk
medications.doNotUseIf: Children/teens with viral illness, aspirin allergy, bleeding disorders, ulcers/GI bleeding, blood thinners, severe liver/kidney disease, late pregnancy
medications.interactions: Avoid combining with other NSAIDs or alcohol unless directed
medications.seekCareIf: Bleeding, black stool, vomiting blood, ringing in ears, allergic reaction, breathing trouble

medications.name: Bacitracin topical
medications.usedFor: Small superficial cuts/scrapes after cleaning
medications.sideEffects: Skin irritation, allergic rash
medications.doNotUseIf: Deep/puncture wounds, animal bites, serious burns, large areas, dirty wounds needing medical care
medications.interactions: Avoid eyes; petroleum ointment may be enough for many minor wounds
medications.seekCareIf: Infection signs, spreading redness, swelling, pus, fever, allergic reaction

medications.name: Benzocaine oral topical
medications.usedFor: Short-term mouth sore or tooth/gum pain relief while arranging dental care
medications.sideEffects: Numbness, irritation; rare but serious methemoglobinemia
medications.doNotUseIf: Children under 2, history of methemoglobinemia, allergy to local anesthetics, severe infection/swelling
medications.interactions: Use lowest amount; do not rely on it for dental infection
medications.seekCareIf: Blue/gray lips or skin, shortness of breath, fatigue, confusion, swelling, fever

medications.name: Benzoyl peroxide
medications.usedFor: Whiteheads, inflamed acne, acne prevention
medications.sideEffects: Dryness, peeling, redness, burning; bleaches fabric/hair
medications.doNotUseIf: Very sensitive skin, allergy to benzoyl peroxide, severe cystic acne needing clinician
medications.interactions: Start slowly; sunscreen/moisturizer can reduce irritation
medications.seekCareIf: Severe swelling, blistering, breathing trouble, severe irritation

medications.name: Bisacodyl
medications.usedFor: Short-term constipation relief
medications.sideEffects: Cramps, diarrhea, nausea, abdominal discomfort
medications.doNotUseIf: Bowel obstruction, severe abdominal pain, nausea/vomiting, rectal bleeding
medications.interactions: Do not crush/chew enteric tablets; avoid milk/antacids close to tablet dosing
medications.seekCareIf: No bowel movement after use, rectal bleeding, severe pain

medications.name: Bismuth subsalicylate
medications.usedFor: Mild diarrhea, nausea, heartburn, indigestion
medications.sideEffects: Black tongue/stool, constipation, ringing in ears
medications.doNotUseIf: Aspirin/salicylate allergy, children/teens with viral illness, bleeding disorders, ulcers, blood thinners, gout, kidney disease, late pregnancy
medications.interactions: Contains salicylate; avoid combining with aspirin/NSAIDs unless directed
medications.seekCareIf: Ringing in ears, bleeding, severe constipation, fever/bloody diarrhea, dehydration

medications.name: Calamine lotion
medications.usedFor: Itching and oozing from poison ivy/oak/sumac or minor skin irritation
medications.sideEffects: Dryness, mild irritation
medications.doNotUseIf: Deep wounds, serious burns, eye/mucous membrane use
medications.interactions: Shake well; external use only
medications.seekCareIf: Rash spreads widely, face/genitals involved, fever, infection signs

medications.name: Calcium carbonate
medications.usedFor: Fast relief of occasional heartburn, acid indigestion, sour stomach
medications.sideEffects: Constipation, gas; high use can cause high calcium/kidney stone risk
medications.doNotUseIf: Kidney disease, history of kidney stones, high calcium, frequent/severe heartburn, trouble swallowing, black stool
medications.interactions: Can reduce absorption of thyroid medicine, iron, some antibiotics; separate doses
medications.seekCareIf: Chest pain, trouble swallowing, vomiting blood, black stool, symptoms >2 weeks

medications.name: Capsaicin topical
medications.usedFor: Localized arthritis, muscle, joint, or nerve-type pain
medications.sideEffects: Burning, stinging, redness; may worsen before improving
medications.doNotUseIf: Broken/irritated skin, near eyes/mucous membranes, capsaicin allergy
medications.interactions: Wash hands; avoid heating pads and hot showers right after applying
medications.seekCareIf: Severe burning, blistering, swelling, or pain does not improve

medications.name: Carbamide peroxide otic
medications.usedFor: Earwax softening/removal when symptoms match wax buildup
medications.sideEffects: Bubbling sensation, mild irritation, temporary decreased hearing
medications.doNotUseIf: Ear pain, drainage, bleeding, ear tubes, perforated eardrum, ear surgery, dizziness
medications.interactions: Do not put objects/cotton swabs deep in ear canal
medications.seekCareIf: Pain, dizziness, discharge, hearing loss persists

medications.name: Cetirizine
medications.usedFor: Sneezing, runny nose, itchy/watery eyes, itching/hives
medications.sideEffects: Drowsiness, tiredness, dry mouth, headache
medications.doNotUseIf: Kidney/liver disease, pregnancy/breastfeeding, or if treating hives with bruising/blistering
medications.interactions: Alcohol/sedatives can increase drowsiness
medications.seekCareIf: Breathing trouble, facial swelling, severe hives, symptoms persist/worsen

medications.name: Chlorpheniramine
medications.usedFor: Cold/allergy runny nose, sneezing, itchy eyes/nose
medications.sideEffects: Drowsiness, dry mouth, constipation, blurred vision, urinary retention
medications.doNotUseIf: Glaucoma, enlarged prostate, urinary retention, breathing problems, older adults, sedative use
medications.interactions: Avoid alcohol/sedatives and driving until effects are known
medications.seekCareIf: Severe drowsiness, confusion, trouble urinating, allergic reaction

medications.name: Clotrimazole topical
medications.usedFor: Itchy/scaly fungal rash such as athlete's foot, ringworm, jock itch
medications.sideEffects: Burning, stinging, redness, irritation
medications.doNotUseIf: Open wounds, eye use, uncertain diagnosis, diabetes/poor circulation with foot infection unless clinician involved
medications.interactions: Use full course even if better; keep area dry
medications.seekCareIf: Spreading redness, pus, fever, no improvement after course

medications.name: Clotrimazole vaginal
medications.usedFor: Symptoms consistent with yeast infection, especially if previously diagnosed
medications.sideEffects: Vaginal burning, irritation, abdominal cramps
medications.doNotUseIf: First infection, pregnancy, pelvic pain, fever, foul discharge, recurrent infections, STI concern
medications.interactions: May affect latex barrier products depending on formulation
medications.seekCareIf: No improvement, symptoms recur, fever/pelvic pain/foul discharge

medications.name: Cromolyn sodium nasal spray
medications.usedFor: Preventing allergy symptoms before exposure; frequent sneezing/runny nose
medications.sideEffects: Sneezing, nasal burning/stinging, bad taste
medications.doNotUseIf: Wheezing, breathing trouble, fever, sinus pain/infection symptoms
medications.interactions: Works best when used before allergen exposure and several times daily per label
medications.seekCareIf: Symptoms worsen, wheezing, facial pain, fever, thick discharge

medications.name: Dextromethorphan
medications.commonExamples: Delsym, Robitussin DM, many cold/cough products
medications.usedFor: Dry, irritating cough without lots of mucus
medications.sideEffects: Drowsiness, dizziness, nausea; misuse can be dangerous
medications.doNotUseIf: MAOI use within 14 days, serotonergic medications unless clinician approves, chronic cough from smoking/asthma/emphysema unless directed
medications.interactions: Avoid alcohol/sedatives; check combo products for duplicate ingredients
medications.seekCareIf: Cough lasts >7 days, returns, fever/rash/headache, breathing trouble

medications.name: Diclofenac sodium topical gel
medications.commonExamples: Voltaren Arthritis Pain Gel, store-brand diclofenac gel
medications.usedFor: Localized osteoarthritis pain in joints such as hands, wrists, elbows, feet, ankles, knees
medications.sideEffects: Skin irritation, itching, dryness; NSAID-like risks are lower but still possible
medications.doNotUseIf: NSAID allergy, before/after heart surgery, late pregnancy, or using on damaged skin
medications.interactions: Avoid using with oral NSAIDs unless clinician says; avoid heat/occlusive wraps over treated area
medications.seekCareIf: Severe rash, swelling, chest pain, shortness of breath, black stool, no improvement

medications.name: Dimenhydrinate
medications.usedFor: Motion sickness nausea/vomiting prevention
medications.sideEffects: Drowsiness, dry mouth, blurred vision, constipation
medications.doNotUseIf: Glaucoma, enlarged prostate/urination problems, breathing disease, seizure disorder, older adults
medications.interactions: Avoid alcohol/sedatives and driving
medications.seekCareIf: Severe drowsiness/confusion, trouble urinating, persistent vomiting/dehydration

medications.name: Diphenhydramine
medications.usedFor: Allergy symptoms, itching/hives, motion sickness, or occasional sleep aid depending on product
medications.sideEffects: Strong drowsiness, dry mouth, constipation, blurred vision, urinary retention, confusion
medications.doNotUseIf: Glaucoma, enlarged prostate/urination problems, breathing problems, older adults, pregnancy/breastfeeding unless clinician approves
medications.interactions: Avoid alcohol, sedatives, driving; avoid combining with other sleep/allergy products
medications.seekCareIf: Confusion, fast heartbeat, trouble urinating, severe dizziness, breathing trouble

medications.name: Docusate sodium
medications.usedFor: Stool softening when straining/hard stools are an issue
medications.sideEffects: Mild cramps, diarrhea, throat irritation with liquid
medications.doNotUseIf: Bowel obstruction, nausea/vomiting, severe abdominal pain, mineral oil use unless directed
medications.interactions: May be less effective than other constipation options for many people
medications.seekCareIf: Rectal bleeding, no bowel movement, severe pain

medications.name: Doxylamine
medications.usedFor: Occasional insomnia only, not chronic sleep problems
medications.sideEffects: Drowsiness, dry mouth, constipation, blurred vision, confusion, urinary retention
medications.doNotUseIf: Glaucoma, enlarged prostate/urination problems, breathing disease, older adults, pregnancy/breastfeeding unless directed
medications.interactions: Avoid alcohol/sedatives and driving; do not use for chronic insomnia without evaluation
medications.seekCareIf: Confusion, trouble urinating, severe dizziness, symptoms persist >2 weeks

medications.name: Esomeprazole
medications.usedFor: Frequent heartburn occurring 2 or more days per week
medications.sideEffects: Headache, nausea, diarrhea, gas, abdominal pain
medications.doNotUseIf: Same PPI red flags: trouble swallowing, GI bleeding signs, chest pain, unexplained weight loss, liver disease, pregnancy/breastfeeding unless directed
medications.interactions: OTC course is typically 14 days; ask about interactions with anticoagulants/antiplatelets and other medications
medications.seekCareIf: Symptoms continue after 14 days, return soon, or red-flag symptoms occur

medications.name: Famotidine
medications.usedFor: Heartburn after meals or nighttime heartburn; prevention before trigger foods
medications.sideEffects: Headache, dizziness, constipation/diarrhea
medications.doNotUseIf: Kidney disease, trouble/pain swallowing, vomiting blood, black stool, heartburn with sweating/chest pain, symptoms >3 months
medications.interactions: Dose adjustment may be needed in kidney disease
medications.seekCareIf: Chest pain, black stool, vomiting blood, symptoms continue >14 days

medications.name: Fexofenadine
medications.usedFor: Sneezing, runny nose, itchy/watery eyes with minimal drowsiness for many people
medications.sideEffects: Headache, nausea, dizziness
medications.doNotUseIf: Kidney disease, pregnancy/breastfeeding
medications.interactions: Fruit juice and aluminum/magnesium antacids can reduce absorption
medications.seekCareIf: Allergic reaction, severe dizziness, symptoms persist/worsen

medications.name: Fluticasone nasal spray
medications.usedFor: Allergy-related nasal congestion, sneezing, runny/itchy nose
medications.sideEffects: Nosebleeds, nasal burning/irritation, headache, sore throat
medications.doNotUseIf: Recent nasal surgery/injury, nasal sores/infection, glaucoma/cataracts, immune suppression, pregnancy/breastfeeding
medications.interactions: Takes several days for full benefit; point spray away from septum to reduce nosebleeds
medications.seekCareIf: Severe nosebleeds, vision changes, persistent irritation, symptoms not improving

medications.name: Guaifenesin
medications.usedFor: Chest congestion and thick mucus where loosening phlegm would help
medications.sideEffects: Nausea, vomiting, dizziness, headache
medications.doNotUseIf: Persistent/chronic cough from smoking/asthma/emphysema or cough with excessive phlegm unless directed
medications.interactions: Drink fluids; avoid duplicate guaifenesin combo products
medications.seekCareIf: Cough lasts >7 days, fever, rash, persistent headache, shortness of breath

medications.symptomUseCase: Itchy rash, insect bites, eczema flare
medications.name: Hydrocortisone 1% topical
medications.usedFor: Minor itching/inflammation from rash, eczema, insect bites, poison ivy
medications.sideEffects: Burning, dryness, irritation; skin thinning with overuse
medications.doNotUseIf: Fungal/bacterial infection, deep wounds, serious burns, acne/rosacea, near eyes, long use on face/genitals unless directed
medications.interactions: Do not cover tightly unless label says; avoid long-term frequent use
medications.seekCareIf: Rash worsens, infection signs, no improvement within label timeframe

medications.name: Ibuprofen
medications.usedFor: Headache, menstrual cramps, toothache, muscle aches, fever, inflammatory pain
medications.sideEffects: Stomach upset, heartburn, dizziness; can increase bleeding, blood pressure, kidney risk
medications.doNotUseIf: NSAID allergy/asthma reaction, stomach ulcer/bleeding, kidney disease, heart disease, taking blood thinners, late pregnancy, or before/after heart surgery
medications.interactions: Higher risk with alcohol, steroids, anticoagulants, other NSAIDs, ACE inhibitors/diuretics
medications.seekCareIf: Chest pain, weakness, black/bloody stool, vomiting blood, swelling, shortness of breath, severe rash

medications.name: Ketoconazole 1% shampoo
medications.usedFor: Dandruff with itching/flaking when antifungal shampoo is appropriate
medications.sideEffects: Scalp irritation, dryness/oiliness, hair texture changes
medications.doNotUseIf: Broken/inflamed scalp, allergy, pregnancy/breastfeeding unless directed
medications.interactions: Avoid eyes; rinse well
medications.seekCareIf: Severe irritation, rash, no improvement

medications.name: Ketotifen ophthalmic
medications.usedFor: Itchy, watery eyes from allergies
medications.sideEffects: Burning/stinging, headache, dry eyes
medications.doNotUseIf: Eye pain, vision changes, infection, contact lens irritation
medications.interactions: Remove contact lenses before use and follow label timing before reinserting
medications.seekCareIf: Eye pain, vision changes, worsening redness/swelling

medications.symptomUseCase: Emergency contraception after unprotected sex/contraceptive failure
medications.name: Levonorgestrel emergency contraception
medications.commonExamples: Plan B One-Step, Take Action, My Way
medications.usedFor: Reducing chance of pregnancy after unprotected sex or birth control failure; sooner is better
medications.sideEffects: Nausea, fatigue, headache, cramps, breast tenderness, menstrual changes
medications.doNotUseIf: Known pregnancy, unexplained vaginal bleeding, allergy to ingredient; ask clinician about weight/BMI effectiveness questions or sexual assault/STI care
medications.interactions: Not routine contraception and does not protect against STIs
medications.seekCareIf: Severe abdominal pain, no period within expected time, pregnancy symptoms

medications.name: Lidocaine topical
medications.commonExamples: Aspercreme Lidocaine, Icy Hot Lidocaine, store-brand lidocaine
medications.usedFor: Localized minor pain, itching, sunburn discomfort, minor burns or insect bites depending on product label
medications.sideEffects: Numbness, burning, stinging, skin irritation
medications.doNotUseIf: Large areas, deep wounds, badly damaged skin, serious burns, lidocaine/local anesthetic allergy
medications.interactions: Too much topical anesthetic can be dangerous; avoid applying heat unless label allows
medications.seekCareIf: Irregular heartbeat, dizziness, confusion, severe irritation, allergic reaction

medications.name: Loperamide
medications.usedFor: Short-term watery diarrhea without red flags
medications.sideEffects: Constipation, dizziness, abdominal cramps; high doses can cause serious heart rhythm problems
medications.doNotUseIf: Bloody/black stool, fever, suspected food poisoning/bacterial diarrhea, C. difficile/antibiotic-associated diarrhea, acute ulcerative colitis, significant abdominal swelling
medications.interactions: Do not exceed label dosing; avoid with drugs that increase heart rhythm risk unless clinician advises
medications.seekCareIf: Fever, blood, severe abdominal pain, dehydration, diarrhea >2 days, fainting/palpitations

medications.name: Loratadine
medications.usedFor: Sneezing, runny nose, itchy/watery eyes with less expected drowsiness
medications.sideEffects: Headache, dry mouth, tiredness; drowsiness is less common
medications.doNotUseIf: Liver/kidney disease, pregnancy/breastfeeding
medications.interactions: Check multi-symptom allergy products for added decongestants
medications.seekCareIf: Allergic reaction, severe dizziness, symptoms persist/worsen

medications.name: Magnesium hydroxide / aluminum hydroxide antacids
medications.usedFor: Fast relief of occasional heartburn/sour stomach
medications.sideEffects: Diarrhea with magnesium; constipation with aluminum; nausea
medications.doNotUseIf: Kidney disease, magnesium restriction, severe abdominal pain, frequent heartburn, black stool
medications.interactions: Can affect absorption of many medicines; separate doses when needed
medications.seekCareIf: Chest pain, black stool, vomiting blood, persistent symptoms

medications.name: Meclizine
medications.commonExamples: Dramamine Less Drowsy, Bonine, store-brand meclizine
medications.usedFor: Motion sickness prevention/treatment; dizziness/vertigo symptoms when appropriate
medications.sideEffects: Drowsiness, dry mouth, blurred vision, constipation
medications.doNotUseIf: Glaucoma, enlarged prostate/urination problems, breathing disease, older adults, pregnancy/breastfeeding unless directed
medications.interactions: Avoid alcohol/sedatives and driving until effects are known
medications.seekCareIf: Severe dizziness, fainting, confusion, persistent vomiting, neurologic symptoms

medications.name: Menthol lozenges
medications.usedFor: Mild sore throat or cough irritation
medications.sideEffects: Mouth/throat irritation, choking risk in young children; sugar-free products may cause GI upset
medications.doNotUseIf: Young children who cannot safely use lozenges, severe sore throat, breathing/swallowing trouble
medications.interactions: Check sugar content in diabetes; do not exceed label use
medications.seekCareIf: Fever, rash, severe or persistent sore throat, difficulty swallowing/breathing

medications.name: Miconazole topical
medications.usedFor: Fungal rash such as athlete's foot, ringworm, jock itch
medications.sideEffects: Burning, stinging, irritation
medications.doNotUseIf: Open wounds, eye use, uncertain diagnosis, diabetes/poor circulation with foot infection unless clinician involved
medications.interactions: Use full course; avoid tight/moist clothing when possible
medications.seekCareIf: No improvement, spreading infection, fever, pus

medications.name: Miconazole vaginal
medications.usedFor: Itching/burning with thick white discharge consistent with prior yeast infection
medications.sideEffects: Vaginal burning, irritation, cramps, headache
medications.doNotUseIf: First yeast infection, pregnancy, fever, pelvic/abdominal pain, foul-smelling discharge, STI concern, diabetes/immunocompromise unless clinician advises
medications.interactions: May weaken latex condoms/diaphragms during use depending on formulation
medications.seekCareIf: No improvement, symptoms return quickly, fever/pelvic pain, bad-smelling discharge

medications.name: Naproxen sodium
medications.usedFor: Backache, arthritis-type pain, cramps, headache, fever when longer dosing interval is preferred
medications.sideEffects: Stomach upset, heartburn, dizziness; can increase bleeding, blood pressure, kidney risk
medications.doNotUseIf: Same major NSAID cautions as ibuprofen: ulcer/bleeding history, kidney disease, heart disease, blood thinners, late pregnancy, NSAID allergy
medications.interactions: Do not combine with ibuprofen/aspirin for pain unless directed; higher bleeding risk with alcohol/blood thinners
medications.seekCareIf: Black stool, vomiting blood, chest pain, severe stomach pain, swelling, breathing trouble

medications.name: Neomycin/polymyxin B/bacitracin
medications.usedFor: Small superficial cuts/scrapes after cleaning
medications.sideEffects: Skin irritation; neomycin allergy/contact dermatitis is common
medications.doNotUseIf: Neomycin allergy, deep/puncture wounds, animal bites, serious burns, large areas
medications.interactions: Do not overuse; stop if rash/itching worsens
medications.seekCareIf: Infection signs, allergic rash, swelling, breathing trouble

medications.name: Nicotine gum/lozenge
medications.usedFor: Short-acting relief of nicotine cravings during quit attempt
medications.sideEffects: Mouth/throat irritation, hiccups, nausea, jaw soreness with gum
medications.doNotUseIf: Recent heart attack, serious arrhythmia, worsening angina, pregnancy/breastfeeding, under 18 unless clinician directs
medications.interactions: Coffee/acidic drinks can reduce absorption if used too close; do not chew gum like regular gum
medications.seekCareIf: Chest pain, palpitations, severe dizziness, overdose symptoms

medications.name: Nicotine patch
medications.usedFor: Helping quit smoking/vaping by reducing nicotine withdrawal
medications.sideEffects: Skin irritation, vivid dreams, insomnia, dizziness, nausea
medications.doNotUseIf: Recent heart attack, serious arrhythmia, worsening angina, pregnancy/breastfeeding, under 18 unless clinician directs
medications.interactions: Do not smoke while using if label warns; remove before MRI if patch contains metal
medications.seekCareIf: Chest pain, irregular heartbeat, severe rash, overdose symptoms

medications.name: Omeprazole
medications.usedFor: Frequent heartburn occurring 2 or more days per week
medications.sideEffects: Headache, nausea, diarrhea, abdominal pain; long-term use has additional risks
medications.doNotUseIf: Trouble/pain swallowing, vomiting blood, black stool, chest pain, unexplained weight loss, liver disease, pregnancy/breastfeeding unless directed
medications.interactions: OTC course is typically 14 days; interacts with some drugs such as clopidogrel/warfarin; ask pharmacist
medications.seekCareIf: Symptoms continue after 14 days, return soon, or red-flag symptoms occur

medications.name: Oral rehydration solution
medications.usedFor: Fluid loss from diarrhea/vomiting, sweating, or mild dehydration
medications.sideEffects: Usually minimal; too much sodium/fluid can matter in some diseases
medications.doNotUseIf: Heart failure, kidney disease, fluid/salt restriction, severe dehydration symptoms
medications.interactions: Not a diarrhea stopper; replaces water/electrolytes
medications.seekCareIf: No urination, confusion, fainting, blood in stool/vomit, persistent vomiting

medications.name: Oxymetazoline nasal spray
medications.usedFor: Severe nasal congestion when short-term fast relief is needed
medications.sideEffects: Nasal burning, stinging, dryness; rebound congestion if used too long
medications.doNotUseIf: Heart disease, high blood pressure, thyroid disease, diabetes, enlarged prostate, MAOI use
medications.interactions: Do not use more than 3 days unless clinician directs
medications.seekCareIf: Congestion worsens, nosebleeds, fast heartbeat, severe dizziness

medications.name: Permethrin 1% lotion/rinse
medications.usedFor: Head lice treatment
medications.sideEffects: Itching, redness, burning, scalp irritation
medications.doNotUseIf: Ragweed/chrysanthemum allergy caution varies by product, infants/pregnancy/breastfeeding unless directed
medications.interactions: Nit combing and household measures matter; repeat treatment may be needed per label
medications.seekCareIf: Scalp infection, eye exposure, breathing trouble, treatment failure

medications.symptomUseCase: Dry/cracked skin, minor wound moisture barrier
medications.name: Petrolatum
medications.usedFor: Dry skin, chapped lips, minor skin barrier protection
medications.sideEffects: Usually minimal; greasy feel, folliculitis rarely
medications.doNotUseIf: Deep/infected wounds, serious burns, allergy to product ingredients
medications.interactions: External use only; avoid contamination of jar/tube
medications.seekCareIf: Infection signs, worsening rash, no healing

medications.name: Phenazopyridine
medications.commonExamples: AZO Urinary Pain Relief, store-brand phenazopyridine
medications.usedFor: Temporary urinary pain/burning relief while seeking care for possible UTI
medications.sideEffects: Orange/red urine, headache, stomach upset
medications.doNotUseIf: Kidney disease, liver disease, pregnancy unless directed, symptoms without medical evaluation
medications.interactions: Does not treat infection; OTC use is usually limited to short duration per label
medications.seekCareIf: Fever, back pain, blood in urine, symptoms >2 days, yellowing skin/eyes

medications.name: Phenol throat spray
medications.usedFor: Temporary relief of minor sore throat/mouth irritation
medications.sideEffects: Mouth/throat numbness, irritation, bad taste
medications.doNotUseIf: Severe sore throat, sore throat with high fever/rash/nausea/vomiting, children unless label permits
medications.interactions: Do not overuse or swallow large amounts
medications.seekCareIf: Sore throat severe or lasts >2 days, fever, headache, rash, swelling

medications.symptomUseCase: Purposes Pain reliever/fever reducer Cough suppressant Nasal decongestant Anorectal/Hemorrhoidal Active ingredients (in each 10 mL) Purposes Acetaminophen 325 mg Pain reliever/fever reducer Diphenhydr…
medications.name: Phenylephrine Hydrochloride
medications.commonExamples: Daytime Cold and Flu, DCH Hemorrhoidal, Childrens Delsym Cough Plus Cold Night Time, DCH Rapid Relief Hemorrhoidal, basic care daytime severe cold and flu, Good Sense Daytime non drowsy cold and flu
medications.usedFor: Uses temporarily relieves these symptoms due to a cold minor aches and pains headache nasal and sinus congestion sore throat cough due to minor throat and bronchial irritation temporarily reduces fever For the temporary relief of pain, soreness, and burning. Helps relieve the local itching and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids. Temporarily shrinks hemorrhoidal tissue. Temporarily provides a coating for the relief of anorectal discomforts. Temporarily protects the inflamed, irritated anorectal surface to help make bowel movements less painful. Uses temporarily relieves these common cold an…
medications.doNotUseIf: Do not use if you are now taking a prescription monoamine oxidase inhibitor (MAOI) (certain drugs for depression, psychiatric or emotional conditions, or Parkinson's disease) or for 2 weeks after stopping the MAOI drug. If you do not know if your prescription drug contains an MAOI, ask a doctor or pharmacist before taking this product. with any other drug containing acetaminophen (prescription or non-prescription). If you are not sure whether a drug contains acetaminophen, ask a doctor or pharmacist. Warnings Liver warning: This product contains acetaminophen. Severe liver damage may occur if…
medications.interactions: Drug Interaction Precautions Do not use if you are now taking a prescription monoamine oxidase inhibitor (MAOI). (certain drugs for depression, psychiatric or emotional conditions, or Parkinson`s disease), or 2 weeks after stopping the MAOI drug. If you are uncertain whether your presciption drug contains an MAOI, consult a health professional before taking this product
medications.seekCareIf: Stop use and ask a doctor if nervousness, dizziness, or sleeplessness occur pain, cough, or nasal congestion gets worse or lasts more than 7 days fever gets worse or lasts more than 3 days redness or swelling is present new symptoms occur cough comes back or occurs with a rash or headache that lasts. These could be signs of a serious condition. Stop use and ask a doctor if nervousness, dizziness, or sleeplessness occur pain, nasal congestion, or cough gets worse or lasts more than 5 days fever gets worse or lasts more than 3 days redness or swelling is present new symptoms occur cough comes ba…

medications.name: Phenylephrine oral
medications.usedFor: Intended for stuffy nose, but FDA has proposed removing oral phenylephrine from OTC monograph because available data show it is not effective as a nasal decongestant
medications.sideEffects: Nervousness, dizziness, increased blood pressure, insomnia
medications.doNotUseIf: High blood pressure, heart disease, thyroid disease, diabetes, glaucoma, enlarged prostate/urination problems, MAOI use
medications.interactions: Check label: phenylephrine nasal sprays are different from oral phenylephrine
medications.seekCareIf: Chest pain, fast heartbeat, severe dizziness, symptoms persist

medications.name: Polyethylene glycol 3350
medications.usedFor: Occasional constipation when a gentle osmotic laxative is preferred
medications.sideEffects: Bloating, gas, nausea, cramps, diarrhea
medications.doNotUseIf: Bowel obstruction, severe abdominal pain, nausea/vomiting, sudden bowel habit changes >2 weeks
medications.interactions: May take 1-3 days; drink fluids
medications.seekCareIf: Rectal bleeding, no bowel movement after use, severe pain, dehydration

medications.name: Pseudoephedrine
medications.usedFor: Stuffy nose/sinus pressure from cold or allergies
medications.sideEffects: Jitteriness, insomnia, fast heartbeat, increased blood pressure, nervousness
medications.doNotUseIf: High blood pressure, heart disease, thyroid disease, diabetes, glaucoma, enlarged prostate/urination problems, MAOI use
medications.interactions: Sold behind-the-counter with purchase limits; avoid late-day dosing if insomnia
medications.seekCareIf: Chest pain, fast/irregular heartbeat, severe dizziness, symptoms >7 days or fever

medications.name: Psyllium fiber
medications.usedFor: Hard stools/constipation where bulk-forming fiber is appropriate
medications.sideEffects: Gas, bloating, abdominal fullness
medications.doNotUseIf: Swallowing difficulty, bowel obstruction, severe abdominal pain, sudden bowel habit changes, inadequate fluid intake
medications.interactions: Must take with a full glass of water to reduce choking/obstruction risk
medications.seekCareIf: Chest pain, vomiting, trouble swallowing/breathing, rectal bleeding

medications.name: Pyrethrins/piperonyl butoxide
medications.usedFor: Head lice treatment
medications.sideEffects: Scalp itching, redness, swelling; allergic reactions
medications.doNotUseIf: Ragweed/chrysanthemum allergy, infants, eye exposure, asthma/respiratory sensitivity caution
medications.interactions: Use only as directed; avoid open flames with some products until hair is dry
medications.seekCareIf: Breathing trouble, severe irritation, infection signs, treatment failure

medications.name: Pyrithione zinc
medications.usedFor: Dandruff flakes/itchy scalp
medications.sideEffects: Scalp irritation, dryness/oiliness
medications.doNotUseIf: Broken/inflamed scalp, allergy, eye exposure
medications.interactions: Use regularly per label for control
medications.seekCareIf: Severe irritation, rash, no improvement

medications.symptomUseCase: Acne, warts, corns/calluses depending on product
medications.name: Salicylic acid topical
medications.usedFor: Acne exfoliation or wart/corn removal depending on product strength/form
medications.sideEffects: Burning, peeling, dryness, irritation
medications.doNotUseIf: Diabetes/poor circulation for foot wart/corn products, irritated/infected skin, large areas, aspirin allergy caution
medications.interactions: Different strengths are for different uses; do not use wart remover on acne/face unless label says
medications.seekCareIf: Severe irritation, infection signs, dizziness/ringing in ears from overuse

medications.name: Saline nasal spray/rinse
medications.commonExamples: Simply Saline, Ocean, NeilMed saline products
medications.usedFor: Dry nasal passages, mild congestion, mucus loosening, nasal rinsing
medications.sideEffects: Temporary burning/stinging; rinse products can cause infection if contaminated
medications.doNotUseIf: For rinses, avoid using tap water unless boiled/cooled or sterile/distilled
medications.interactions: Use clean devices and proper water to reduce infection risk
medications.seekCareIf: Severe pain, fever, worsening symptoms, nosebleeds

medications.name: Selenium sulfide
medications.usedFor: Dandruff/flaking/itching scalp
medications.sideEffects: Scalp irritation, oiliness/dryness, hair discoloration
medications.doNotUseIf: Broken/inflamed scalp, allergy, use near eyes
medications.interactions: Rinse well; follow frequency on label
medications.seekCareIf: Severe irritation, rash, no improvement

medications.name: Senna / sennosides
medications.usedFor: Short-term occasional constipation when stimulant laxative is needed
medications.sideEffects: Cramps, diarrhea, nausea, urine discoloration
medications.doNotUseIf: Bowel obstruction, severe abdominal pain, nausea/vomiting, sudden bowel habit changes, pregnancy/breastfeeding unless directed
medications.interactions: Avoid long-term daily use unless clinician directs
medications.seekCareIf: Rectal bleeding, no bowel movement after use, severe cramps/dehydration

medications.name: Simethicone
medications.usedFor: Gas pressure, bloating, belching
medications.sideEffects: Usually minimal; nausea/constipation/diarrhea can occur rarely
medications.doNotUseIf: Severe abdominal pain, persistent vomiting, swollen abdomen, symptoms suggesting bowel obstruction
medications.interactions: Treats gas bubbles, not the underlying cause of severe pain
medications.seekCareIf: Severe or persistent abdominal pain, fever, vomiting, bloody stool

medications.name: Terbinafine topical
medications.usedFor: Athlete's foot, ringworm, some jock itch products depending on label
medications.sideEffects: Itching, burning, stinging, dryness
medications.doNotUseIf: Nail/scalp fungal infections, open wounds, uncertain diagnosis, pregnancy/breastfeeding unless directed
medications.interactions: Not for all fungal infections; follow label duration
medications.seekCareIf: No improvement, worsening rash, allergic reaction

medications.name: Tetrahydrozoline or naphazoline eye drops
medications.commonExamples: Visine Original, Clear Eyes redness relievers
medications.usedFor: Temporary cosmetic redness relief only when no red flags
medications.sideEffects: Burning/stinging, pupil dilation, rebound redness with overuse
medications.doNotUseIf: Glaucoma, heart disease, high blood pressure, eye pain, injury, infection, vision changes
medications.interactions: Avoid routine use; redness relievers can mask serious eye problems
medications.seekCareIf: Eye pain, vision changes, redness lasts >72 hours or worsens

medications.name: Tolnaftate
medications.usedFor: Athlete's foot, jock itch, ringworm depending on product
medications.sideEffects: Skin irritation, dryness, burning
medications.doNotUseIf: Open wounds, eye use, uncertain diagnosis
medications.interactions: Keep area clean/dry; powders can help moisture control
medications.seekCareIf: No improvement after course, worsening redness/swelling

medications.name: Triamcinolone nasal spray
medications.usedFor: Allergy-related nasal congestion, sneezing, runny/itchy nose
medications.sideEffects: Nosebleeds, nasal irritation, headache
medications.doNotUseIf: Recent nasal surgery/injury, glaucoma/cataracts, infections, pregnancy/breastfeeding
medications.interactions: Use consistently; do not exceed label dosing
medications.seekCareIf: Severe nosebleeds, vision changes, symptoms persist/worsen

symptomLookup.considerFirst: Acetaminophen if liver/alcohol cautions do not apply
symptomLookup.otherOptions: Ibuprofen or naproxen if inflammation and NSAIDs are safe
symptomLookup.avoidRedFlags: Seek care for very high fever, stiff neck, confusion, dehydration, chest pain, severe pain, or symptoms in high-risk patients
symptomLookup.notes: Check cold/flu products for hidden acetaminophen/APAP

symptomLookup.considerFirst: Ibuprofen or naproxen if NSAIDs are safe
symptomLookup.otherOptions: Acetaminophen if NSAIDs are not safe; topical diclofenac for localized arthritis
symptomLookup.avoidRedFlags: Avoid NSAIDs with ulcers/GI bleeding, kidney disease, blood thinners, late pregnancy, serious heart disease unless clinician approves

symptomLookup.otherOptions: Fluticasone/triamcinolone nasal spray for nasal congestion; cromolyn for prevention
symptomLookup.avoidRedFlags: Seek care for wheezing, facial swelling, severe hives, or eye pain/vision changes

symptomLookup.otherOptions: Oxymetazoline nasal spray for max 3 days; nasal steroid if allergy-related
symptomLookup.avoidRedFlags: Avoid decongestants with uncontrolled hypertension, heart disease, thyroid disease, glaucoma, BPH/urination problems, MAOIs unless clinician approves
symptomLookup.notes: FDA has proposed removing oral phenylephrine for lack of effectiveness

symptomLookup.otherOptions: Honey for adults/children over 1 year; lozenges for throat irritation
symptomLookup.avoidRedFlags: Seek care for shortness of breath, chest pain, coughing blood, fever, or cough >7 days
symptomLookup.notes: Avoid dextromethorphan with MAOIs and use caution with serotonergic meds

symptomLookup.avoidRedFlags: Seek care for trouble breathing, chest pain, high fever, bloody sputum, or chronic lung disease flare
symptomLookup.notes: Expectorants loosen mucus but do not cure infection

symptomLookup.considerFirst: Menthol lozenges or phenol spray for temporary relief
symptomLookup.avoidRedFlags: Seek care for trouble breathing/swallowing, drooling, rash, high fever, severe one-sided pain, or symptoms >2 days with fever

symptomLookup.otherOptions: Loperamide only if no fever/blood; bismuth subsalicylate if salicylate-safe
symptomLookup.avoidRedFlags: Do not use loperamide for bloody diarrhea, fever, suspected infection, or antibiotic-associated diarrhea

symptomLookup.considerFirst: Polyethylene glycol 3350 or psyllium with fluids
symptomLookup.otherOptions: Senna/bisacodyl short-term stimulant laxatives; docusate for hard stools
symptomLookup.avoidRedFlags: Seek care for severe abdominal pain, vomiting, bloating, rectal bleeding, sudden bowel habit change, or no bowel movement after laxative
Severe abdominal pain, fever, vomiting, swollen belly, or bloody stool needs care

symptomLookup.considerFirst: Calcium carbonate antacid for fast relief
symptomLookup.otherOptions: Famotidine for prevention/longer relief; omeprazole/esomeprazole for frequent heartburn only
symptomLookup.avoidRedFlags: Chest pain, black stool, vomiting blood, trouble swallowing, unexplained weight loss, or symptoms >14 days need care
Avoid if driving or using alcohol/sedatives; use caution in glaucoma/BPH/older adults

symptomLookup.considerFirst: Hydrocortisone 1% or calamine depending on rash
symptomLookup.otherOptions: Oral antihistamine for itching if safe
symptomLookup.avoidRedFlags: Seek care for infection signs, facial/genital involvement, widespread rash, breathing trouble, or severe allergic reaction
Diabetes/poor circulation foot symptoms, spreading infection, pus, fever, or no improvement need care
Severe cystic acne, scarring, or pregnancy/planning pregnancy with retinoids should get clinician input

symptomLookup.otherOptions: Bacitracin or triple antibiotic if desired and no allergy
symptomLookup.avoidRedFlags: Deep/puncture wounds, animal bites, dirty wounds, burns, infection signs need care
Eye pain, injury, vision changes, severe redness, or discharge needs care
Eye pain, vision change, infection, or contact lens-related pain needs care
symptomLookup.notes: Remove contacts before use per label

symptomLookup.considerFirst: Carbamide peroxide drops if no ear red flags
symptomLookup.avoidRedFlags: Do not use with ear pain, drainage, bleeding, ear tubes, perforated eardrum, dizziness
symptomLookup.notes: Avoid cotton swabs deep in ear

symptomLookup.considerFirst: Phenazopyridine for short-term pain relief only
symptomLookup.avoidRedFlags: Fever, back/flank pain, pregnancy, blood in urine, male urinary symptoms, symptoms >2 days need care

symptomLookup.considerFirst: Miconazole or clotrimazole vaginal antifungal if previously diagnosed and symptoms match
symptomLookup.avoidRedFlags: Pregnancy, pelvic pain, fever, foul discharge, STI concern, recurrent symptoms need care
symptomLookup.notes: May weaken latex products depending on formulation

symptomLookup.considerFirst: Levonorgestrel emergency contraception as soon as possible
symptomLookup.otherOptions: Ask clinician/pharmacist about other EC options
symptomLookup.avoidRedFlags: Severe abdominal pain later or missed period/pregnancy symptoms need testing/care
Recent MI, serious arrhythmia, worsening angina, pregnancy/breastfeeding, under 18: ask clinician

sources.layer1: ClinMedFind conventional OTC symptom guide (homeopathic and home remedies excluded)
